elements.name: Title
texts: A boat trip to Saints Island

elements.name: Description
texts: A description of Lough Derg and its Pilgrimage in the American Donahoe's Magazine

elements.name: Source
texts: Lough Derg and Its Pilgrimages', in Donahoe's Magazine, vol. 4, p. 316

elements.name: Contributor
texts: Digitised by Google, sponsored by University of Wisconsin-Madison, archived on Hathi Trust digital library

elements.name: Text
texts: "Before drawing to a conclusion, I should not forget to mention that at two o'clock each afternoon the pilgrims' boat starts for Saints' Island, which is about two miles distant from Station Island ; and a more delightful little trip can hardly be imagined. During the voyage the pilgrims while away the time most agreeably by singing litanies and hymns, and occasionally the sound of instrumental music may be heard. Having traversed Saints' Island, they start on their homeward trip, after having spent about two hours in this charming little excursion."
